briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — steady winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and stable conditions.

KVD: Flatside crankbait (Hardliner 35) is the FIRST crankbait in extreme cold — 'the difference between a flatside and even a squarebill is night and day in the amount of bites in extremely cold water.' Smaller profile, no rattle, but flat sides give off huge pressure waves fish home in on.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: Flatside crankbait (Hardliner 35) is the FIRST crankbait in extreme cold — 'the difference between a flatside and even a squarebill is night and day in the amount of bites in extremely cold water
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: For the shallow zone in cold water, use a flatside crankbait (Hardliner). Extremely tight action — bass use their lateral line to hunt in cold water and flatsides displace a ton of water. Small p
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'I'm a big believer in flat-sided crankbaits in winter — the KVD 1.5 Flat and Lucky Shad for cold water. Throw Lucky Shad on spinning equipment with 8lb fluoro for extra distance and depth.'

Robertson: Spring crankbait progression -- 1) Prespawn: slow-roll lipless on flats near channel banks in 1-6 feet; 2) Postspawn: big-bodied squarebill on tapering points near spawning flats in under 10 feet; 3) Late postspawn: deep diver following...

Wheeler: Big fish spots are big giant flats in 22-29 ft with tiny isolated rockpiles — a boulder in the middle of a rocky flat, or a hard-bottom spot. Only good for 1-2 fish and time-consuming to find, but that's what separates you

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable winter conditions position bass at 15-35ft on main lake structure — bluffs, steep points, channel bends. Fish are lethargic and grouped tight. Vertical presentations directly on structure are essential.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
